Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|UNKNOWN}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - consideration that it was enshrined by descendants of iron-producing peoples. {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましこうほくくしんよしたちよう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:港北区の歴史]] [[Category:港北区の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|しんよした]] [[Category:高田・新吉田]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - consideration that it was enshrined by descendants of iron-producing peoples. {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましこうほくくしんよしたちよう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:港北区の歴史]] [[Category:港北区の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|しんよした]] [[Category:高田・新吉田]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - consideration that it was enshrined by descendants of iron-producing peoples. {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましこうほくくしんよしたちよう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:港北区の歴史]] [[Category:港北区の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|しんよした]] [[Category:高田・新吉田]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=UNKNOWN}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - consideration that it was enshrined by descendants of iron-producing peoples. {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましこうほくくしんよしたちよう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:港北区の歴史]] [[Category:港北区の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|しんよした]] [[Category:高田・新吉田]] [[Category:Qsort]]{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}[[Category:qq]] [[Category:qqq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184|1=Shin-yoshida-chō}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village|1=Nitta}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292|1=Tsurumi River}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423|1=Hayabuchi River}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama|1=Tenjin Jinja}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival|1=Reisai}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329|1=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294|1=Higashi-Yamata Station}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601|1=Kurayami Matsuri}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298|1=Sugiyama Shrine (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163|1=Tsurumi Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292|1=Tsurumi River}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423|1=Hayabuchi River}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama|1=Tenjin Jinja}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival|1=Reisai}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329|1=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294|1=Higashi-Yamata Station}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}} - Haiden
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184|1=Shin-yoshida-chō}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village|1=Nitta}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292|1=Tsurumi River}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423|1=Hayabuchi River}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama|1=Tenjin Jinja}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival|1=Reisai}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329|1=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294|1=Higashi-Yamata Station}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601|1=Kurayami Matsuri}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298|1=Sugiyama Shrine (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163|1=Tsurumi Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184|1=Shin-yoshida-chō}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village|1=Nitta}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292|1=Tsurumi River}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423|1=Hayabuchi River}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama|1=Tenjin Jinja}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival|1=Reisai}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329|1=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294|1=Higashi-Yamata Station}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601|1=Kurayami Matsuri}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298|1=Sugiyama Shrine (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163|1=Tsurumi Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - consideration that it was enshrined by descendants of iron-producing peoples. {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:すきやましんしや よこはましこうほくくしんよしたちよう}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:港北区の歴史]] [[Category:港北区の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|しんよした]] [[Category:高田・新吉田]] {{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:QSugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}{{DEFAULTSORT:出羽神社Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)}}[[Category:qq]]

{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|32|46.3|N|139|36|29.3|E}}}} |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}{{ill|lt=Shin-yoshida-chō|Q97180184|WD=Q97180184|1=Shin-yoshida-chō}}4509 |位置 = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|32|46.33|N|139|36|29.38|E|region:JP-14_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} |社格 = Shikinaisha small shrine, former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = |本殿 = |別名 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Yokohama|label=Sugiyama Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} |}} '''Sugiyama Shrine''' (すぎやまじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in {{ill|lt=Kōhoku Ward|Q1358965|WD=Q1358965|1=Kōhoku-ku, Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Yokohama|Q38283|WD=Q38283|1=Yokohama}}, {{ill|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|Q127513|WD=Q127513|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}. It was the former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} of Yoshida in old {{ill|lt=Nitta Village (Kanagawa)|Q11503739|WD=Q11503739|lt=Nitta Village|1=Nitta}}, and is regarded as one of the strong candidate shrines for "{{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}}," the only shikinaisha in Yokohama and in former {{ill|lt=Musashi Province|Q907398|WD=Q907398|1=Musashi Province}}. === Enshrined deity === * {{ill|UNKNOWN|WD=Q10881556|lt=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto|1=Isotakeru-no-Mikoto}} (Iso-takeru) * {{ill|lt=co-enshrined deities|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|lt=Co-enshrined|1=Saijin}}: {{ill|lt=Kamakura Kagemasa|Q1154948|WD=Q1154948|lt=Gongorō Kagemasa|1=Kamakura Gongorō Kagemasa}}, {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hino Seibyō|1=UNKNOWN}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|lt=Susanoo-no-Mikoto|WD=Q272993|1=Susanoo-no-Mikoto}}[http://kamnavi.jp/it/musasi/indexy.htm#khayabu1 Musashi's Isotakeru no Mikoto, Shin-yoshida-chō Sugiyama Shrine] (Kamnavi) *: In {{ill|lt=1908|Q2056|WD=Q2056|1=1908}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 41), three shrines in Nitta village were {{ill|lt=merged|Q11412524|WD=Q11412524|1=Co-Enshrinement}}[http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines). === Origin and history === There is a theory that it was worshipped by a clan that moved from {{ill|lt=Bōsō Peninsula|Q1022115|WD=Q1022115|1=Bōsō Peninsula}}. Its founding time is unknown. ''Musashi Shikishaki'' states, "In Tsuzuki District, at the place called Yoshida, there is a small place-name Sugiyama, and the shrine there is the true site." ''{{ill|lt=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|Q11503943|WD=Q11503943|1=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki-kō}}'' (compiled in the Bunka-Bunsei period, 1804-1829) records: "Tax-exempt land 2 dan 5 se; to the north of the village; several stone steps up to a torii before the shrine; main shrine 2 ken by 2.5 ken; haiden 3 ken by 2 ken, south-facing; sacred body immovable; held by Shōfuku-ji in the village" {{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}. Sacred treasures and historical documents of this shrine had been kept by Shōfuku-ji, its former {{ill|lt=bettō-ji|Q11396755|WD=Q11396755|1=Bettō-ji}}, but are said to have been completely lost in a fire at that temple[http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha of Musashi Tsuzuki District] (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom). In {{ill|lt=1873|Q7757|WD=Q7757|1=1873}} (Meiji 6), it was ranked {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} in then Tsuzuki District's Yoshida Village (which became Nitta Village in the 1889 merger). === As a shikinaisha candidate === The theory identifying this shrine as the shikinaisha predates the Bunka-Bunsei period when ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' was compiled. Works such as ''Historical Geography of Musashi Shikinaisha'' by late-Edo Shinto scholar Saitō Yoshihiko and local historian Hishinuma Isamu (published 1966, revised 1972 as ''Ancient Shrines of Musashi'') treat this theory as strong[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku52/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 52nd article, strong candidate for Sugiyama Shrine] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Hishinuma cited the existence of a local minor place-name "Sugiyama," oral tradition in the long-established Mori family connected to this shrine claiming over 1,000 years of history, and a kofun called Asamazuka behind the Mori residence indicating a once-powerful local clan. ''History of Kōhoku Ward'' also says the Mori family name derives from "chinju-no-mori" and that the family is locally called "Sugiyama-sama"[http://www.okuraken.or.jp/depo/chiikijyouhou/kouhoku_rekishi_bunka/kouhoku53/ History and Culture of Kōhoku Ward: 53rd article, Sugiyama shrines in the ward] (Ōkura Institute for Spiritual Culture). Nearby in Hayabuchi 3-chōme are the Kitagawa Omote-no-Ue site and {{ill|lt=Kitagawa shell mound|Q11401676|WD=Q11401676|1=Kitagawa Shell Mound}}, and local memory of a "Kaizuka-batake" aligns with the ''Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō'' note that there was a large shell mound in Tsuzuki District Yoshida village{{sfn|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}, showing ancient sites in this area have long been known. Like many other candidate shrines, this shrine stands on elevated ground along the {{ill|lt=Tsurumi River|Q7850292|WD=Q7850292|1=Tsurumi River}} system ({{ill|lt=Hayabuchi River|Q11511423|WD=Q11511423|1=Hayabuchi River}}). === Precinct shrines === * {{ill|lt=Inari Shrine|Q514480|WD=Q514480|lt=Inari-sama|1=Inari shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tenjin Shrine|Q11443287|WD=Q11443287|lt=Tenjin-sama|1=Tenjin Jinja}} === Festivals and annual events === * {{ill|lt=Annual festival|Q11385469|WD=Q11385469|lt=Annual Grand Festival|1=Reisai}}: {{ill|lt=October 9|Q2923|WD=Q2923|1=October 9}} (autumn grand festival) === Other === No priest is stationed permanently at this shrine; it is managed concurrently by [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=359&cd=1203043&scd=000&npg=0 Hachiman-sha] in Setogaya-chō, Hodogaya Ward. === Location and access === 4509 Shin-yoshida-chō, Kōhoku Ward, Yokohama, Kanagawa Prefecture * {{ill|lt=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line|Q910329|WD=Q910329|1=Yokohama Municipal Subway Green Line}}: 15-20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Higashi-Yamata Station|Q11103294|WD=Q11103294|1=Higashi-Yamata Station}} === Gallery === File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 01.JPG|Front (south) torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 04.JPG|Northwest-side torii File:Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, shinyoshidacho) 10.JPG|Inari-sama and Tenjin-sama in precincts === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{Reflist}} === References === * {{cite book|和書|title=Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō|volume=Vol.84 Tsuzuki District No.4|chapter=Yoshida Village|publisher=Geographic Bureau, Ministry of Home Affairs|date=1884-6|id={{NDLJP|763987/56}}|ref={{sfnref|Shinpen Musashi Fudoki Kō Yoshida Village}}}} === Related items === {{commonscat|Sugiyama jinjya (Shrine, Shinyoshida-cho)}} * {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine|Q135039726|WD=Q135039726|1=Sukiyamano Shrine}} - several dozen are distributed mainly in Yokohama; besides this shrine, major candidates include: ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Midori-ku Nishihassaku-chō)|Q11521945|WD=Q11521945|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} - one of the six major shrines of Bushū, also participates in {{ill|lt=Kurayami Festival|Q11265601|WD=Q11265601|1=Kurayami Matsuri}} (Musashi provincial festival). ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Chigasaki Chūō)|Q17994298|WD=Q17994298|1=Sugiyama Shrine (Shrine, Chigasaki-chuo)}} ** {{ill|lt=Sugiyama Shrine (Yokohama Tsuzuki-ku Nakagawa)|Q17994300|WD=Q17994300|1=Sugiyama Shrine}} * {{ill|lt=Tsurumi Shrine (Yokohama)|Q11676163|WD=Q11676163|1=Tsurumi Shrine}} === External links === * [http://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/search_dtl.php4?jid=176&cd=1202081&scd=&npg=0 Sugiyama Shrine] (Kanagawa Prefectural Association of Shinto Shrines) * [http://www.akanedai.net/sugiyama_23.php Sugiyama Shrine (Shin-yoshida-chō)] (Akanedai.net) * {{Wayback|url=http://www18.ocn.ne.jp/~tugaru/sikinaisyasugiyama.html |title=Inference on Sugiyama Shrine, the only shikinaisha in Musashi Tsuzuki District |date=20140810041733}} (Tsugaru Ancient Kingdom) - consideration that it was enshrined by descendants of iron-producing peoples. {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shinto shrine]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Sugiyama Shrine (Q17994294)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinai Shōsha]] [[Category:Sugiyama Shrine]] [[Category:Shikinai Ronsha]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Musashi Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:横浜市の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:港北区の歴史]] [[Category:港北区の建築物]] [[Category:武蔵国の式内社]] [[Category:杉山神社|しんよした]] [[Category:高田・新吉田]] [[Category:qq]]
